action.reasoning: Observation: Training fails with persistent 'Image features and image tokens do not match (tokens: 240, features: 240)' after debug print despite matching counts; both train.py and predict.py truncated in workspace (incomplete loop/optimizer/save/metrics/logging/save in train.py; incomplete point parsing/JSON output in predict.py). test_prep confirms ~7k samples with bbox/point in abs pixels. Analysis: Truncation means executed train.py lacks full training logic after __getitem__ debug (hence error on incomplete batch/forward); dual processor calls with non-list args + variable screenshot patches can cause internal placeholder mask mismatch in Qwen2VL even if counts print equal. Root fix: complete non-truncated scripts using lists for all processor calls, manual bs=1 no-DataLoader loop (safe for variable patches), robust full-conversation template + prompt_length calc, smaller max_pixels=128*28*28 for stability/speed, complete AdamW loop with metrics.jsonl logging, full adapter+processor+grid save. Complete predict.py with all fallback parsers, full point/bbox rescale (from 1000-grid), bound clipping, center default, and exact JSON output per eval contract. This follows standard Qwen2VL grounding SFT and will yield first successful baseline. Decision: Overwrite train.py/predict.py with FULL complete short code (128 samples/32 steps ~30s), run test_prep+train, submit_eval for first grounding_score + time before scaling data/epochs/LR/rank.
